2604.09155.title: CORA: Conformal Risk-Controlled Agents for Safeguarded Mobile GUI Automation
2604.09155.paper_summary: Graphical user interface (GUI) agents powered by vision language models (VLMs) are rapidly moving from passive assistance to autonomous operation. However, this unrestricted action space exposes users to severe and irreversible financial, privacy or social harm. Existing safeguards rely on prompt engineering, brittle heuristics and VLM-as-critic lack formal verification and user-tunable guarantees. We propose CORA (COnformal Risk-controlled GUI Agent), a post-policy, pre-action safeguarding framework that provides statistical guarantees on harmful executed actions. CORA reformulates safety as selective action execution: we train a Guardian model to estimate action-conditional risk for each proposed step. Rather than thresholding raw scores, we leverage Conformal Risk Control to calibrate an execute/abstain boundary that satisfies a user-specified risk budget and route rejected actions to a trainable Diagnostician model, which performs multimodal reasoning over rejected actions to recommend interventions (e.g., confirm, reflect, or abort) to minimize user burden. A Goal-Lock mechanism anchors assessment to a clarified, frozen user intent to resist visual injection attacks. To rigorously evaluate this paradigm, we introduce Phone-Harm, a new benchmark of mobile safety violations with step-level harm labels under real-world settings. Experiments on Phone-Harm and public benchmarks against diverse baselines validate that CORA improves the safety--helpfulness--interruption Pareto frontier, offering a practical, statistically grounded safety paradigm for autonomous GUI execution. Code and benchmark are available at cora-agent.github.io.
2604.09155.paper_summary_zh: 圖形用戶界面（GUI）代理由視覺語言模型（VLMs）驅動，正迅速從被動輔助轉向自主操作。<lb/>然而，這種不受限制的行動空間使得用戶面臨嚴重且不可逆的財務、隱私或社會傷害。<lb/>現有的安全措施依賴於提示工程、脆弱的啟發式方法以及VLM作為評價者的缺乏正式驗證和用戶可調整的保證。<lb/>我們提出了CORA（COnformal Risk-controlled GUI Agent），這是一種後政策、前行動的安全框架，提供對有害執行行動的統計保證。<lb/>CORA將安全性重新定義為選擇性行動執行：我們訓練一個Guardian模型來估計每個提議步驟的行動條件風險。<lb/>我們不僅僅是對原始分數進行閾值處理，而是利用符合風險控制來校準滿足用戶指定風險預算的執行/放棄邊界，並將被拒絕的行動路由到可訓練的Diagnostician模型，該模型對被拒絕的行動進行多模態推理，以建議干預措施（例如，確認、反思或中止）以減少用戶負擔。<lb/>一個Goal-Lock機制將評估固定在明確、凍結的用戶意圖上，以抵抗視覺注入攻擊。<lb/>為了嚴格評估這一範式，我們引入了Phone-Harm，一個新的移動安全違規基準，具有在現實世界設置下的步驟級傷害標籤。<lb/>在Phone-Harm和公共基準上進行的實驗，與各種基準進行比較，驗證了CORA改善了安全性-有用性-中斷的Pareto邊界，為自主GUI執行提供了一個實用的、統計基礎的安全範式。<lb/>代碼和基準可在cora-agent.github.io獲得。
2604.09155.author: Yushi Feng et.al.
2604.09155.authors: Yushi Feng, Junye Du, Qifan Wang, Zizhan Ma, Qian Niu, Yutaka Matsuo, Long Feng, Lequan Yu
